notes: Baseline: 12 log-spaced distances, width=256 depth=4 relu
8 even-spaced: training diverged (NaN loss), likely too few inputs or data issue
12 even-spaced: uniform spacing dramatically beats log-spacing (37% improvement)
20 log-spaced: more distances with log-spacing worse than baseline
20 even-spaced: more distances uniform, still worse than 12 even
16 even-spaced: intermediate count, worse than 12 even
8 even-spaced: better than log-baseline but worse than 12 even (51.8% off best)
12 even + arc features: no benefit from radius/direction info (+51.9% vs best)
28 even-spaced: more distances hurt, 12 is sweet spot
4 even-spaced: too few distances, worst result
gelu activation: 42% better than relu — smooth activation matches problem structure
width=512: 48% better than w256 baseline — more capacity helps
depth=5 with relu: much worse — too deep for this problem with relu
w512+gelu: gelu helps but width still not bottleneck at depth=4
w512+d5+gelu: larger model with gelu, still worse than w256+d3+gelu
w256+d3+gelu: 25% improvement — shallower depth=3 with gelu is optimal
w512+d3+gelu: wider helps but still has dropout=0.1, worse than no-dropout
w256+d2+gelu: too shallow (depth=2), worse
w256+d3+gelu+drop=0: removing dropout key insight, 59% improvement
w512+d3+gelu+nodrop: wider not better than w256+d4
w256+d4+gelu+nodrop: 45% better — depth=4 optimal without dropout
lower lr=3e-4 with d3: converges slower, worse in 500 epochs
ref config+10Kep: w256d5relu+mse+he_normal, 10K epochs — matches reference result
w256d4gelu+mse+10Kep: good but relu+d5 3x better
